output.explanation: Uppercase variant deviates from the dominant lowercase 3-letter code pattern seen in values like "eng", "pol", "fre", "spa".
Deviates from the dominant compact language-code/domain pattern; other values are usually 3-letter lowercase codes or simple language names, not a long semicolon-delimited description.
Concatenates two 3-letter codes without a separator; multi-language values elsewhere use separation such as spaces in "eng     por" or names in "English     Italian".
Long mixed-format value with repeated names and comma text deviates from the column's usual short codes or simple language names/multi-language lists.
Concatenates two codes and uses uppercase, while comparable values use lowercase codes and separation, e.g. "eng     por".
Concatenates two 3-letter codes without a separator; multi-language values elsewhere are separated rather than merged.
Concatenates two 3-letter codes without a separator; compare with separated multi-language value "eng     por".
Capitalization deviates from the dominant lowercase 3-letter code pattern seen in "eng", "pol", "fre".
Long mixed-format value with reversed/repeated language list and comma text deviates from the usual short codes or simple language names/multi-language lists.
Uses a 2-letter code, while the code pattern in the column is predominantly 3-letter codes such as "eng", "pol", "fre", "spa".
Uses a 2-letter code, while the column predominantly uses 3-letter lowercase codes such as "por", "eng", "fre".
